anthropics/anthropic-cookbook#95.body:
In your [blob post](https://www.anthropic.com/news/contextual-retrieval), you mention that your prepend the context to the actual chunk but in the guide notebook it's the opposite. It probably doesn't affect performances but for consistency I thought I would raise it.
 
The line in question is this one ([guide](https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-cookbook/blob/main/skills/contextual-embeddings/guide.ipynb)):
Current:
```python
'text_to_embed': f"{chunk['content']}\n\n{contextualized_text}"
```
Corrected:
```python
'text_to_embed': f"{contextualized_text}\n\n{chunk['content']}"
```
anthropics/anthropic-cookbook#95.title: Nit: prepend contextual text instead of appending it to chunks in guide.ipynb

anthropics/anthropic-cookbook#288.body:
I'm trying to learn about prompt caching. Maybe I'm missing something, but in the `anthropic/claude-cookbooks/misc/data/prompt_caching.ipynb` example, the documentation in the notebook describes a prompt without a prompt_cache and other prompts with a prompt_cache.  The difference seems to be whether the pertinent function contains a "cache_control" entry or not.  

Like this:  `"cache_control": {"type": "ephemeral"},`  

But all examples, whether using prompt-caching or not, contain the cache_control statement, and I don't seen any difference between the two methods.  Could someone who knows about this take a look at the notebook, and maybe add a comment in somewhere if the code is correct about what makes one have prompt caching and the other not have it, or conversely, if the code is incorrect, please provide a correction.

Here is the URL to the notebook:  

<https://github.com/anthropics/claude-cookbooks/blob/main/misc/prompt_caching.ipynb>   

Separately, a quick example of how one can use prompt caching in a conversation via an IDE, or even directly with your Claude.ai web interface would be something I would use with nearly every conversation.

Thanks,  
RL
anthropics/anthropic-cookbook#288.title: prompt_caching example code needs a look

anthropics/claude-cookbooks#303.body:
## Community Resource Submission

**Name:** Claude Agentic Patterns  
**URL:** https://github.com/ThibautMelen/claude-agentic-patterns

## Description

I created a visual reference guide that compiles agentic patterns from Anthropic's official documentation into an accessible format.

### Patterns Covered

1. **Subagent Orchestration** - Task delegation to specialized subagents
2. **Progressive Skills** - On-demand capability loading
3. **Parallel Tool Calling** - Simultaneous tool execution optimization
4. **Master-Clone Architecture** - Self-spawning agents with context inheritance
5. **Multi-Window Context** - State persistence across context windows
6. **Programmatic Orchestration** - Code-driven tool workflows
7. **Wizard-Style Workflows** - Multi-step processes with confirmations

### Features

- 30+ colorful Mermaid diagrams (optimized for dark/light mode)
- Python & TypeScript code examples
- "When to use / When not to use" guidance for each pattern
- Direct links to source documentation

### Sources

All content derived from official Anthropic resources:
- Claude 4.5 Best Practices
- Anthropic Engineering Blog
- Agent SDK Documentation
- Claude Code Documentation

Could potentially be referenced in the cookbook or linked as a community resource.

**License:** MIT
anthropics/claude-cookbooks#303.title: Community Resource: Claude Agentic Patterns - Visual Pattern Guide

anthropics/claude-cookbooks#313.body:
## Context

I've submitted [PR #312](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-cookbooks/pull/312) with enhancements to the `claude_agent_sdk/` tutorial section. This issue provides a detailed breakdown to facilitate review and discussion.

**I'm happy to split this into smaller PRs if the team prefers - just let me know!**

---

## Detailed Breakdown

### 1️⃣ Notebook Enhancements

#### `00_The_one_liner_research_agent.ipynb`
- Added richer output visualization showing tokens, cost, and duration
- Improved activity printing with cleaner formatting
- Better examples demonstrating stateless vs stateful patterns

#### `01_The_chief_of_staff_agent.ipynb`
- Cleaner cell outputs throughout
- Improved flow between sections
- Better demonstration of slash commands and hooks

#### `02_The_observability_agent.ipynb`
- Enhanced MCP server configuration examples
- Better visualization of agent activity
- Cleaner output formatting for GitHub integration demos

---

### 2️⃣ Agent Implementation Improvements

#### `research_agent/agent.py`
- Refactored for cleaner code structure
- Improved `print_activity()` and `print_final_result()` functions
- Better token/cost reporting

#### `observability_agent/agent.py`
- Enhanced visualization capabilities
- Improved error handling
- Better integration with the visualizer utilities

#### `chief_of_staff_agent/agent.py`
- Minor refactoring for clarity
- Updated slash command definitions

---

### 3️⃣ New Utilities

#### `utils/html_renderer.py` (608 lines - NEW)
Provides rich HTML visualization for Jupyter notebooks:
- `HTMLConversationRenderer` - Renders full conversation flows
- `HTMLActivityRenderer` - Real-time activity visualization
- Styled output with CSS for professional appearance
- Collapsible sections for tool calls and results

#### `utils/agent_visualizer.py` (Refactored)
- Major cleanup for maintainability
- Separated concerns between console and HTML output
- Improved `visualize_conversation()` function

---

### 4️⃣ New Documentation

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `docs/README.md` | Entry point explaining the docs structure |
| `docs/ADDITIONS.md` | Comprehensive list of all enhancements made |
| `docs/CODEBASE_OVERVIEW.md` | Architecture guide - how the tutorial components fit together |
| `docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md` | Common issues and solutions for users working through tutorials |

**Rationale:** The existing tutorials are excellent but lack supporting documentation. These docs help newcomers:
- Understand the overall architecture before diving in
- Troubleshoot common issues independently
- See what was enhanced and why

---

### 5️⃣ Configuration

#### `pyproject.toml`
- Added `pyyaml` dependency (used for configuration parsing)

---

## Questions for Maintainers

1. **PR Size**: Would you prefer this split into separate PRs (notebooks, agents, utils, docs)?
2. **Documentation**: Is the `docs/` structure appropriate, or do you have a preferred format?
3. **html_renderer.py**: Is this utility welcome, or does it overlap with existing tooling?

---

## Files Changed Summary

```
19 files changed, +5,225 lines, -686 lines

Notebooks:     3 files modified
Agents:        3 files modified  
Utilities:     2 files (1 new, 1 refactored)
Documentation: 4 files (all new)
Config:        1 file modified
Commands:      4 files modified
Other:         2 files modified
```

Looking forward to your feedback!
anthropics/claude-cookbooks#313.title: [Discussion] Claude Agent SDK Tutorial Enhancements - PR #312 Breakdown
